content.claim: BTMN (integration, learning, behavioral difficulty) is a distinct Hungarian legal category defined in Act CXC of 2011, legally milder than SNI, with 2.1% prevalence among preschool children (2018/2019) but 9.5% among school-age children—a nearly five-fold jump attributed by sources to either genuine emergence of difficulties at school entry or differential screening sensitivity between settings.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Last publicly available data is from 2018/2019; no continuous time series for BTMN growth is provided
Sources flag but do not adjudicate between genuine emergence versus screening-bias explanations
BTMN is not internationally standardized, making cross-national comparison difficult
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
